claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I have one child - my son Alex, who is 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Now I need to ask you a few more questions about your situation to determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. Alex lives with me here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great. Now I need to ask you:

**Are you responsible for Alex? For example, do you provide for his day-to-day care, food, clothes, and other needs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I look after Alex full-time - I take care of all his day-to-day needs, his food, clothes, everything he needs really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or any residential accommodation for an extended period, or does he live with someone else for any reason?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. He lives at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 6 years old (under 16)
- You live with Alex in the UK (Manchester)
- You are responsible for his care and provide for all his needs
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- He lives with you full-time

You meet all the requirements for Child Benefit. You can now proceed to make a claim if you haven't already done so.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), so he meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for his day-to-day care, providing for his food, clothes, and all his needs. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex lives at home full-time with the claimant and is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. According to the eligibility rules, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit as they are responsible for a child under 16 and live with them in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 6), who lives with them in Manchester. The claimant is fully responsible for Alex's care and upkeep, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and there are no special circumstances (such as Alex being in hospital or care). The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), so he meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for his day-to-day care, providing for his food, clothes, and all his needs. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex lives at home full-time with the claimant and is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. According to the eligibility rules, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit as they are responsible for a child under 16 and live with them in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 6), who lives with them in Manchester. The claimant is fully responsible for Alex's care and upkeep, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and there are no special circumstances (such as Alex being in hospital or care). The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
